title: ANZTOX data processing
text: processing implemented single consolidated script: script prepares ecotoxicology data ANZTOX database Species Sensitivity Distribution (SSD) analysis — statistical method used derive Guideline Values (DGVs) water quality. pipeline five main stages: 1. Helpers — Three normalisation functions defined ensure consistent matching keys: normalize_cas strips CAS numbers digits , normalize_mediatype maps variants “Freshwater” “Marine”, normalize_name lowercases strips punctuation chemical names. 2. Inputs — Two external CSV files read: CAS parent lookup table (mapping chemical variants/salts parent compound) master DGV table April 2026 (published guideline values validate ). 3. Database extraction — Connects local PostgreSQL infogathering database, reads toxicityvalue2000 toxicityvalue2016 tables, de-normalises joining lookup tables (species, chemical, mediatype, endpoint, effect, testtype, etc.) produce two clean flat tables. 4. SSD eligibility & nesting — two clean datasets combined, chemicals grouped parent CAS via lookup, filtered keep valid concentration records. test type priority rule applied (chronic preferred subchronic, subchronic acute, per species/chemical/endpoint group). Concentrations geometric-mean averaged per species endpoint, sensitive endpoint per species selected. chemical×mediatype combinations ≥5 species ≥4 major groups retained (minimum SSD thresholds). nested list-column ready SSD fitting. 5. DGV matching — SSD-eligible chemicals matched back published 2000 DGVs see many historical guidelines can reproduced database data. summary table categorises matches unmatched DGVs likely derivation method.

title: ssddata — Copilot Instructions
text: Three datasets exported package:

title: ssddata — Copilot Instructions
text: Acute toxicity records (EC50/LC50) aggregated one geometric mean concentration per species per chemical. Key constraint: chk::check_key(envirotox_acute, c("Chemical", "Species")) — unique per chemical × species.

title: ssddata — Copilot Instructions
text: Chronic toxicity records (NOEC/NOEL) aggregated similarly. Key constraint: chk::check_key(envirotox_chronic, c("Chemical", "Species")).

title: ssddata — Copilot Instructions
text: Chemical lookup table joining two datasets .

title: ssddata — Copilot Instructions
text: build script (modified Yanagihara et al. 2024 code) processes envirotox.xlsx (three sheets: test, substance, taxonomy): Filter records: acute = EC50/LC50 Test.type == ""; chronic = NOEC/NOEL Test.type == "C"; exclude records Effect..5X..water.solubility == "1" Unit conversion: mg/L → µg/L (× 1000) Geometric mean per original.CAS × Latin.name using EnvStats::geoMean() Minimum species/group thresholds: ≥6 species, ≥2 trophic groups per chemical Bimodality coefficient (BC) via mousetrap::bimodality_coefficient(log10(Conc)) — used Yanagihara24 flag Group normalisation: "Invert" → "Invertebrate", "Amphib" → "Amphibian", sentence case OriginalCAS dropped acute/chronic saving; kept envirotox_chemical

title: ssddata — Copilot Instructions
text: adding envirotox data ssddata: Prefix: envirotox (e.g., envirotox_acute, envirotox_chronic, envirotox_chemical) Column mapping: ssddata uses Conc (µg/L), Species, Group — matches envirotox columns directly; Chemical column individual ssddata datasets (chemical encoded dataset name per-chemical sets) Aggregate datasets: envirotox_acute envirotox_chronic already multi-chemical aggregates; treat like ccme_data / aims_data — doc files end "envirotox_acute" / "envirotox_chronic" Source file: place envirotox.xlsx data-raw/envirotox/ build script data-raw/envirotox/create_envirotox.R Connors2019 — EnviroTox database paper (doi:10.1002/etc.4382) Yanagihara2024 — distribution comparison paper (doi:10.1016/j.ecoenv.2024.116379) Iwasaki2025 — model-averaging paper (doi:10.1093/etojnl/vgae060)

what: Key Difference from Other ssddata Sources
title: ssddata — Copilot Instructions
text: Unlike CCME/AIMS/CSIRO datasets (one dataset per chemical), envirotox datasets pre-aggregated multi-chemical tables Chemical column acting grouping variable. Yanagihara24 Iwasaki25 logical flags allow subsetting published benchmark subsets without creating separate datasets.

title: Species Sensitivity Distribution Data
text: ssddata R package Species Sensitivity Distribution (SSD) benchmark datasets can serve reference standard testing evaluation SSD methodologies. package includes range data sets sourced Canadian Council Ministers Environment (ccme), Australian Institute Marine Science (aims), Commonwealth Scientific Industrial Research Organisation (csiro), Australian New Zealand water water quality guideline website (angz, datasets published date later 2000), well anonymous datasets supplied various parties (anon). source dataset indicated using pre-fix data name (e.g. ccme, aims, etc), actual chemical name following (e.g. ccme_boron). package also includes wqbench_data, large data set US EPA ECOTOX database, cleaned standardized wqbench package; envirotox_data, dataset based envirotox database; anztox_data, based ANZTOX database historically hoasted SETAC AU website. Please see relevant reference page info information dataset. can browse source code github. Top report error, request features data additions please post github issues page.

title: ANZTOX Species Sensitivity Data — anztox_data
text: curated dataset species-level ecotoxicity data compiled ANZTOX database harmonised Species Sensitivity Distribution (SSD) analysis. Data encompass two source datasets: toxicityvalue2000 (ANZECC & ARMCANZ 2000 guidelines, 17,755 records) toxicityvalue2016 (post-2000 ANZG revision data, 2,794 records).

title: ANZTOX Species Sensitivity Data — anztox_data
code: anztox_data

title: ANZTOX Species Sensitivity Data — anztox_data
text: tibble one row per chemical × mediatype combination columns: casnumber_grouped Grouped CAS number (character). chemicalname_grouped Grouped chemical name (character). mediatype "Freshwater" "Marine" (character). data List-column nested tibbles containing species-level toxicity data.

title: ANZTOX Species Sensitivity Data — anztox_data
text: data sourced ANZTOX database, originally served SETAC now maintained locally. dataset processed harmonised following principles documented ANZECC & ARMCANZ (2000) Warne et al. (2025). data cleaning SSD eligibility workflow fully documented accompanying vignette: vignette("ANZTOX-data-processing", package = "ssddata") brief: Harmonisation: 2000 2016 datasets—differ structure endpoint vocabulary—de-normalised, cleaned, combined single table. Chemical variants salts mapped parent compounds via curated CAS parent lookup. Endpoint labels 2016 mapped 2000 effect codes. Test type priority: Within species × chemical × endpoint group, chronic data preferred subchronic, subchronic acute. Acute--chronic conversion: acute data available species, acute LC/EC/IC50 values divided default ACR 10 (Warne et al. 2025, Section 3.4.2.2) obtain chronic negligible-effect equivalents SSD fitting. Aggregation: Concentration values species × endpoint geometric-mean averaged. sensitive endpoint per species retained. Eligibility threshold: chemical × media combinations ≥ 5 species ≥ 4 distinct major taxonomic groups retained. nested list-column ready SSD fitting related functions. columns follows: casnumber_grouped grouped chemical CAS number, mapped parent compound applicable (chr). chemicalname_grouped grouped chemical name, mapped parent compound name applicable (chr). mediatype Water type: "Freshwater" "Marine" (chr). data list-column containing tibble species-level rows chemical × media combination. row represents single species contains columns: scientificname, commonname_species, majorgroup, minorgroup, endpoint, endpoint_concentration (geometric mean concentration µg/L mg/L, depending chemical), testtype (Chronic Acute), source_datasets (comma-separated source: "2000", "2016", ), n_acute_converted (count acute records contributed geometric mean ACR conversion).

Implications analysis acute chronic ecotoxicity data.” Ecotoxicology Environmental Safety, 278, 116379. doi:10.1016/j.ecoenv.2024.116379 .  three exceptions: dataset also includes chemicals (1) bimodality coefficient > 0.555, (2) six nine species, (3) two trophic groups. logical column Yanagihara24 indicates whether chemical meets criteria used Yanagihara et al. text: named list three elements: acute tibble 14,949 rows 6 columns acute toxicity records (EC50/LC50). See envirotox_acute full column descriptions. chronic tibble 1,721 rows 5 columns chronic toxicity records (NOEC/NOEL). See envirotox_chronic full column descriptions. chemical tibble 744 rows 2 columns mapping chemical names CAS Registry Numbers. See envirotox_chemical full column descriptions.

title: Species Sensitivity Data from the EnviroTox Database — envirotox_data
text: https://envirotoxdatabase.org/

title: Species Sensitivity Data from the EnviroTox Database — envirotox_data
text: envirotox data contains SSD datasets EnviroTox database 2.0.0 (Connors et al. 2019). datasets provided assessing general patterns SSD data testing code. datasets used draw conclusions toxicity individual chemicals. data aggregated following code provided Yanagihara M, Hiki K, Iwasaki Y (2024). “distribution choose deriving species sensitivity distribution? Implications analysis acute chronic ecotoxicity data.” Ecotoxicology Environmental Safety, 278, 116379. doi:10.1016/j.ecoenv.2024.116379 .  three exceptions: datasets also include chemicals (1) bimodality coefficient > 0.555, (2) six nine species, (3) two trophic groups. logical column Yanagihara24 $acute $chronic indicates chemicals meet original criteria Yanagihara et al. (2024): bimodality coefficient <= 0.555, least 10 species, least three trophic groups. logical column Iwasaki25 $acute indicates chemicals included analysis Iwasaki Y, Yanagihara M (2025). “Comparison model-averaging single-distribution approaches estimating species sensitivity distributions hazardous concentrations 5\% species.” Environmental Toxicology Chemistry, 44(3), 834–840. doi:10.1093/etojnl/vgae060 . : 50 species least three trophic groups, excluding certain metals. full reproducible workflow generate three component datasets data-raw/envirotox/DATASET.R.

title: Species Sensitivity Data from the EnviroTox Database — envirotox_data
text: Connors KA, Beasley , Barron MG, Belanger SE, Bonnell M, Brill JL, de Zwart D, Kienzler , Krailler J, Otter R, Phillips JL, Embry MR (2019). “Creation Curated Aquatic Toxicology Database: EnviroTox.” Environmental Toxicology Chemistry, 38(5), 1062–1073. doi:10.1002/etc.4382 . Yanagihara M, Hiki K, Iwasaki Y (2024). “distribution choose deriving species sensitivity distribution? Implications analysis acute chronic ecotoxicity data.” Ecotoxicology Environmental Safety, 278, 116379. doi:10.1016/j.ecoenv.2024.116379 . Iwasaki Y, Yanagihara M (2025). “Comparison model-averaging single-distribution approaches estimating species sensitivity distributions hazardous concentrations 5\% species.” Environmental Toxicology Chemistry, 44(3), 834–840. doi:10.1093/etojnl/vgae060 .

title: Species Sensitivity Data from US EPA ECOTOX Database — wqbench_data
text: data sourced : U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (2025). “ECOTOXicology Knowledgebase.” Accessed 2025, https://cfpub.epa.gov/ecotox/ (visited ). data cleaning steps closely follow wqbench processing procedures resulting data similar. brief, wqbench processing compiles data ECOTOX database firstly classifying tests either acute chronic; converting acute data chronic based published acute chronic ratios; finally aggregating data single row individual species. Aggregation achieved grouping data life stage; selecting sensitive endpoint; selecting preferred toxicity estimate(s) event one available; applying geometric mean one value preferred toxicity metric available sensitive endpoint. deviations wqbench processing steps applied generalise data set beyond focus British Columbia (BC), part align technical guidance jurisdictions (example, Australia New Zealand, ANZG). Deviations wqbench processing steps include: chemicals six species included regardless whether contain species present BC. excluded wqbench designed aim representative BC. chemicals representative species four taxonomic classes included. done align somewhat methods ANZG (https://www.waterquality.gov.au/anz-guidelines/guideline-values/derive/warne-method-derive-2025) requires guidelines derived taxa four "distinct taxa". Using class define "distinct taxa" approximates requirement, definition provided ANZG linked directly one taxonomic hierarchical level therefore applied automated way using taxonomic hierarchy.
